title: Marketplace Org-Template Contract
description: SSOT JSON-Schema (draft 2020-12) for the Molecule marketplace org-template artifact — the publishable shape of an org-template `org.yaml` (+ per-agent `workspace.yaml`) (molecule-ai-org-template-* repos). DERIVED FROM THE REAL ARTIFACTS + their CI validator: molecule-ai-org-template-molecule-dev/org.yaml (+ community-manager/workspace.yaml), gemini-growth-team, mock-bigorg, validated by validate-org-template.py (required: name; must have at least `workspaces` or `defaults`; each workspace needs `name`; `plugins` must be a list; recurses `children`). TWO COMPOSITION STYLES are tolerated, exactly as the real templates use them: (1) INLINE — `workspaces` is a LIST of recursive workspace nodes with `children[]` (gemini-growth-team, mock-bigorg); (2) FOLDER-TREE — `org.yaml` references per-agent `workspace.yaml` files via custom YAML tags (`!include`, `!external`) that resolve at platform load time to workspace nodes (molecule-dev). Those custom tags are a YAML-load concern (validate-org-template.py parses past them): `!include x.yaml` loads as a plain path STRING and `!external {repo,ref,path}` loads as a plain MAP. `workspaces` therefore accepts BOTH a LIST and a MAP (name->node), and each LIST item is one of THREE forms: a resolved inline workspaceNode, an UNRESOLVED `!external` ref object (`$defs/unresolvedExternalRef`), or an UNRESOLVED `!include` path string. The two unresolved forms are PRE-RESOLUTION refs that core's `!include`/`!external` resolver expands into workspace nodes at platform load time; modelling them lets the real folder-tree templates (molecule-dev, molecule-production) validate as-authored, not only post-resolution. Modelled permissively (additionalProperties:true) because org templates carry heterogeneous per-org config (category_routing, idle-loop knobs, initial prompts).

defaults.description: Org-wide defaults applied to every workspace (runtime, tier, plugins[], category_routing, idle_prompt/idle_interval_seconds, initial_prompt, ...). Per-workspace `plugins:` UNIONs with `defaults.plugins`. Modelled as an OPEN object because the contents are heterogeneous per-org; validate-org-template.py only requires that `workspaces` OR `defaults` is present.

workspaces.description: The workspace tree. Accepts BOTH a LIST of workspace items (inline composition — gemini-growth-team/mock-bigorg) AND a MAP of name->node. In the FOLDER-TREE style (molecule-dev, molecule-production) the real org.yaml does NOT inline the nodes: it lists per-agent references via custom YAML tags — `!include teams/x.yaml` (a path string after YAML load) and `!external {repo, ref, path}` (a map after YAML load). core's `!include`/`!external` resolver EXPANDS these to full workspace nodes at platform load time; they are PRE-RESOLUTION refs, not resolved nodes. So each LIST item is one of THREE forms: (1) a resolved inline workspaceNode; (2) an unresolved `!external` ref object `{repo, ref, path}`; (3) an unresolved `!include` path STRING. The MAP branch carries the resolved name->node style.

oneOf.description: List of workspace items. Each item is EITHER a resolved inline workspaceNode, OR an unresolved pre-resolution ref: an `!external` object `{repo, ref, path}` or an `!include` path string (both expanded by core's resolver at load time).

Unresolved `!include` ref — the path (relative to the template repo root) of a per-agent `workspace.yaml`/`teams/*.yaml`. The custom `!include` YAML tag is stripped at load (the scalar value remains a plain string); core's resolver reads the file and expands it into a workspaceNode.
Map of workspace name -> workspace node.

unresolvedExternalRef.description: An UNRESOLVED `!external` workspace reference, as it appears in a folder-tree org.yaml AFTER the custom `!external` YAML tag is stripped at load time (molecule-dev pins the dev department, molecule-core#105 / internal#77). core's gitops resolver fetches `repo` at `ref` and expands `path` into a workspaceNode at platform import time; this object is the PRE-RESOLUTION ref, not the resolved node. Distinct oneOf branch from workspaceNode (which requires `name`, absent here).

repo.description: Source repo holding the external workspace, e.g. `molecule-ai/molecule-dev-department`.

path.description: Path within `repo`@`ref` to the agent's `workspace.yaml`, e.g. `dev-lead/workspace.yaml`.

workspaceNode.description: A single workspace (agent) node. Recursive via `children[]`. Fields derived from the inline nodes (gemini-growth-team) and the folder-tree workspace.yaml (molecule-dev/community-manager). `name` is the only field validate-org-template.py requires; `plugins` must be a list when present.

role.description: Free-text role description for the agent.

runtime.description: Optional per-workspace runtime override (else inherits `defaults.runtime`). Canonical hyphen form.

files_dir.description: Directory under the template repo holding this agent's files (prompts, schedules).

plugins.description: Per-workspace plugins (UNIONed with defaults.plugins). Must be a list (validate-org-template.py enforces). Bare names or source-contract strings; prefix `!`/`-` to opt a default OUT.

items.description: A plugin name or source-contract string (optionally `!`/`-` prefixed to opt out a default).

channels.description: External channel bindings (e.g. a Discord channel the agent owns).

schedules.description: Per-workspace schedule grid (same family as the workspace-template schedule shape).

initial_prompt.description: Inline prompt run once on first boot (not on restart). Keep local — no A2A sends.

initial_prompt_file.description: Path to the first-boot prompt file (alternative to inline `initial_prompt`).

idle_prompt.description: Inline idle-loop reflection prompt (self-sent while heartbeat.active_tasks == 0). Empty disables the idle loop.

idle_prompt_file.description: Path to the idle-loop prompt file (alternative to inline `idle_prompt`).

idle_interval_seconds.description: Idle-loop interval in seconds (ignored when idle prompt is empty).

canvas.description: Canvas placement coordinates for the org graph UI.

children.description: Child workspace nodes (recursive). The tree depth/fan-out is unbounded (mock-bigorg nests deeply).

runtimeId.description: Canonical runtime identifier (hyphen form). Same SSOT enum as plugin-manifest.schema.json; underscore aliases accepted.

prompt.description: Inline prompt run on the cron tick.

prompt_file.description: Path to a prompt file run on the cron tick.
